title: Soothe Itchy Eyes with These 8 Effective Herbal Tinctures

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues, providing quick relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Kaempferol is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the irritated skin around the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and itching.
Saponins are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm irritation in the eyes.
Tannins are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing quick relief from red and irritated eyes.
Flavonoids are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and calm irritation in the eyes, making them feel more comfortable.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy eyes. The flowers of the Calendula plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This can provide quick relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, irritants, or dry air. The tincture can also help to calm the skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, Calendula tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections in the eyes. When applied to the eyes, the tincture can help to reduce the urge to rub or scratch the eyes, allowing the eyes to heal and recover naturally.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that helps with itchy eyes, you will need a few simple items. Start by gathering fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves, flowers, and stems from a trusted source, making sure they are free from any contaminants. Next, combine one part of the Achillea millefolium plant material with two parts of a high-proof spirit like vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to six weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the plant material infuse into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material. Finally, add a small amount of glycerin or a preservative to the liquid to help it last longer and prevent spoilage. Your Achillea millefolium tincture is now ready to use – simply take a few drops under the tongue as needed to help soothe itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.

Saponins are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness.
Polyphenols are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe the eyes, providing quick relief from itching and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is good for itchy eyes because it has soothing properties that calm the skin around the eyes. This plant-based tincture is known to reduce inflammation and redness, which are common causes of itchy eyes. The aloe vera extract in the tincture works to cool and moisturize the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and irritation. When applied to the eyes, it can help to reduce the itching and burning sensations, allowing the eyes to heal faster. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of aloe vera help to prevent infections and promote a healthy environment around the eyes. By using aloe barbadensis tincture, you can experience quick relief from itchy eyes and get back to your daily activities.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that helps with itchy eyes, start by harvesting fresh or dried yarrow flowers and leaves. You can use a combination of both, but make sure they are clean and dry. Next, add about a cup of the yarrow plant material to a clean glass jar, and cover it with a high-proof spirit like vodka or brandy. The ratio of plant material to liquid is usually 1:3, meaning one part of the plant to three parts of liquid. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the mixture infuse. After the infusing period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, and discard the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed by putting a few drops in a glass of water and applying it to your itchy eyes with a cotton ball.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help relieve itchy eyes. The plant, also known as eye bright, has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tincture is made by extracting the plant's active ingredients, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and redness, making it easier to open your eyes and see clearly. When used to treat itchy eyes, the tincture can also help to reduce the production of excess mucus and discharge, which can contribute to the itching. Additionally, the soothing effects of Euphrasia officinalis tincture can help to calm the nerves and reduce any discomfort or tension around the eyes. As a result, many people find that Euphrasia officinalis tincture provides quick and effective relief from itchy eyes, and it can be used as a natural alternative to conventional eye drops or medications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures that help with itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves. You'll need a clean glass jar with a lid and a high-proof neutral spirit, such as vodka or everclear. Fill the jar about 1/4 full with the dried or fresh plant material, then pour the spirit over it, making sure the plant is completely covered. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking it well every day for 2-4 weeks. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, add a few drops to a cup of water or put it directly into your eyes, but make sure to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have sensitive eyes or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy eyes. This is because it contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When these compounds are applied topically, they can calm the eyes and reduce redness. The antihistamine properties help block the release of histamine, a chemical in the body that causes allergic reactions and itching. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy eyes. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area using a cotton swab or mixed with a carrier oil for a more gentle approach.  Note: Before using any new remedy, especially one that you plan to apply directly to your eyes, consult with a healthcare professional to ensure it is safe for you to use.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that helps with itchy eyes, start by collecting and drying a sufficient amount of the plant's flowers and leaves. You can do this by tying the stems together in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the herbs are completely dry, use a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to finely chop them into a powder. Next, combine the dried and powdered herbs with a high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, making sure the herbs are completely covered by the liquid. The general ratio is one part herbs to three parts liquid, but you can adjust this to your preference. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to small dropper bottles and use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Euphorbia peplus herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tincture is a natural remedy for itchy eyes. The plant, also known as comfrey, has been used for centuries to soothe skin irritations and eye problems. When applied to itchy eyes, the tincture's active compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to calm inflammation and reduce itching. Allantoin is a gentle, non-irritating substance that helps to repair damaged skin and eye tissues, making it an ideal ingredient for soothing itchy, irritated eyes. Rosmarinic acid, on the other hand, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and protect the eyes from further irritation. When used regularly, Symphytum officinale tincture can provide long-lasting relief from itchy eyes and promote a healthy, comfortable eye environment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering dried or fresh Achillea millefolium plant parts, such as leaves and flowers. Clean and chop the plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area. Next, combine one part of the plant material with four parts of a neutral-tasting liquid, like vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. Make sure the plant material is completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the liquid extract the beneficial properties from the plant. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material. Transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture for itchy eyes, dilute it with water according to the label instructions and apply it to the affected area with a cotton ball or swab. However, before using any herbal remedy, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a licensed herbalist to ensure its safety and effectiveness for your specific condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is often used to help with itchy eyes. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The tincture is made from the roots of the Echinacea plant, which is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated tissues. When applied to itchy eyes, Echinacea purpurea tincture can help to calm the itch and reduce redness, making it easier to see and feel more comfortable. The tincture can be applied directly to the eyes with a clean cloth or cotton ball, or it can be added to a warm compress to help soothe the eyes. By reducing inflammation and irritation, Echinacea purpurea tincture can provide quick relief from itchy eyes and help to promote healing and recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tincture for itchy eyes, you'll need dried or fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. First, you'll need to prepare the herbs by chopping or bruising them to release their oils and flavors. Next, fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the prepared herbs. Then, pour a solvent such as 80% to 100% vodka, 95% grain alcohol, or glycerin over the herbs, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is one part herbs to two or three parts solvent. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the herbs' properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. To use the tincture, place a few drops in your eyes or apply a few drops to a cotton ball and gently wipe your eyes, but be sure to follow proper safety precautions and consult with a healthcare professional before using the tincture.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tincture is made from the flowers of the chamomile plant. It's often used to soothe itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you put a few drops of the tincture into your eyes, the active ingredients like apigenin and luteolin calm down the blood vessels in your eyes. This reduces the redness and swelling that can cause itchiness. The tincture also has antihistamine properties, which help to stop the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause allergic reactions and make your eyes itch. By using Chamomilla recutita tincture, you may be able to get relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, irritation, or dryness. The tincture can also help to protect your eyes from further irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture for itchy eyes, start by harvesting the leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as yarrow, when it's in full bloom. Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly to prevent contamination. Next, combine 1 part of the dried yarrow with 2-3 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The solvent helps to extract the active compounds from the plant. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every day for 2-3 weeks to allow the mixture to steep. This process is called maceration. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a small dropper bottle and store it in the fridge to slow down the aging process. When needed, dilute a few drops of the tincture with water and apply it to the affected area around the eyes to help soothe and calm itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tincture can help with itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture is made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which are rich in antioxidants and other helpful compounds. When applied to the eyes, the tincture can soothe and calm the irritated tissues, reducing swelling and redness. This can provide quick relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other irritants. The plant's natural ingredients also have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium tincture can help to alleviate itchy eyes and bring relief to those experiencing discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures that help with itchy eyes, you will need some fresh or dried yarrow flowers, high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar. First, prepare the yarrow flowers by rinsing them with clean water and gently patting them dry. If using fresh flowers, you can chop them into smaller pieces. Fill a clean glass jar with the prepared yarrow flowers, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Next, pour high-proof vodka or glycerin over the yarrow flowers, making sure that they are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Achillea millefolium tincture is now ready to use as a natural remedy for itchy eyes. You can take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water or apply it directly to the affected area, following the recommended dosage and usage guidelines.

intro_desc: Itchy eyes can be a real nuisance, making it hard to focus on daily activities and enjoy the things you love. If left untreated, it can also lead to more serious issues like dryness, redness, and even infections. Herbal tinctures can be a great way to alleviate itchy eyes, and two of the best herbs for this are Calendula officinalis and Aloe barbadensis. Calendula officinalis is a natural anti-inflammatory that can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Aloe barbadensis, on the other hand, is known for its cooling and moisturizing properties, which can help calm and hydrate the eyes, reducing itchiness and irritation. By using a tincture made from these herbs, you can find relief from itchy eyes and get back to your normal activities. Fixing the issue is important because it can improve your quality of life, allowing you to sleep better, work more efficiently, and enjoy your daily routine without the discomfort and distraction of itchy eyes.
supplementary_best: For itchy eyes, the best combination of herbal tinctures is Calendula officinalis and Euphrasia officinalis. Calendula officinalis is a soothing herb that reduces inflammation and calms irritated skin, while Euphrasia officinalis is a natural antihistamine that helps to alleviate itching and redness.  The combination of these two herbs is effective because Calendula officinalis helps to calm the skin and reduce inflammation, making it easier for Euphrasia officinalis to target and reduce the itchy symptoms. Euphrasia officinalis also helps to soothe the eyes and reduce redness, making it ideal for itchy eye relief.  Using a 1:1 ratio of Calendula officinalis to Euphrasia officinalis is recommended. Take 5-7 drops of the combined tincture, three times a day, as needed. You can add a few drops to a glass of water or take it directly under the tongue for faster absorption.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy eyes, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures made from plants that can irritate or exacerbate the condition. For example, tinctures made from goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) should be used with caution, as they contain berberine, which can cause redness and irritation in the eyes. Another herb to be careful with is goldenseal's relative, barberry (Berberis vulgaris), which also contains berberine.  Tinctures made from mullein (Verbascum thapsus) and plantain (Plantago major) might not be the best choices either, as they can be quite drying and may worsen itchy eyes.  Additionally, tinctures made from burdock (Arctium lappa) and dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) root may not be suitable, as they can cause irritation and redness in some people.  It's also worth noting that tinctures made from herbs like stinging nettle (Urtica dioica) and celandine (Chelidonium majus) should be used with caution, as they can cause skin and eye irritation in some individuals.
supplementary_other_remedies: Itchy eyes can be a real nuisance, especially if you can't figure out what's causing them. Besides herbal tinctures, there are other natural remedies that can help soothe and calm itchy eyes. One of these is aloe vera gel. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. When applied directly to the eyes, it can help calm the irritation and itching. This is because aloe vera contains compounds called glycoproteins, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe the eyes.  Another natural remedy for itchy eyes is honey. Yes, you read that right - honey! Honey has antibacterial properties that can help combat eye infections and other underlying causes of itchy eyes. When applied to the eyes, honey can help lock in moisture and soothe the irritation. This is because honey contains hydrogen peroxide, which has antibacterial properties that can help fight off infections.  Cold compresses are also a great way to soothe itchy eyes. By applying a cold compress to the eyes, you can help reduce swelling and redness. The cold temperature can help constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce the itching and inflammation in the eyes.  Finally, some people swear by using tea bags to soothe itchy eyes. Used tea bags, especially those made from green tea, can be applied to the eyes to help reduce inflammation and itching. This is because green tea contains antioxidants that can help combat eye infections and reduce inflammation.  These remedies can be especially helpful if you're experiencing itchy eyes due to allergies or eye infections. However, if your itchy eyes persist or worsen, it's always a good idea to consult with a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions that may need medical attention.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience itchy eyes often have other related health issues. Dry eyes, caused by a lack of tears or poor tear quality, can lead to itchy eyes. To treat dry eyes, herbal remedies like Eyebright can be used. Eyebright is a plant that helps to reduce inflammation and improve tear production.  Another common issue is allergic conjunctivitis, an allergic reaction to dust, pollen, or other particles. Itchy eyes, redness, and swelling are symptoms of this condition. Herbs like Quercetin, a flavonoid found in plants like buckwheat and onions, can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe itchy eyes.  Blepharitis, an inflammation of the eyelids, can also cause itchy eyes. This condition is often caused by a bacterial or demodex mite infestation. Herbs like Goldenseal can be used to treat blepharitis. Goldenseal contains berberine, an alkaloid that has antibacterial properties, which can help to eliminate bacterial infections and soothe itchy eyes.  Additionally, itchy eyes can be a symptom of an underlying condition like rosacea, a chronic skin condition characterized by redness and inflammation. Herbs like Green tea, which contains antioxidants, can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Green tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe itchy eyes and reduce redness.  People with itchy eyes may also experience other health issues like sinusitis, a sinus infection that can cause itchy eyes, redness, and swelling. Herbs like Echinacea, a plant that has anti-inflammatory properties, can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Echinacea can help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of sinusitis.  In these cases, medicinal herbs like Eyebright, Quercetin, Goldenseal, Green tea, and Echinacea can be used to treat the underlying conditions and alleviate symptoms of itchy eyes.
